display_name: Mparanza Retail Data and Mapping Service
trigger: The user selects the installed current-database Retailer Signals workflow, the downstream Brand Fit workflow, or an explicit development fresh-scrape persistence path.

data_sent.when: When requesting or consuming a magic link or using a persisted authenticated session
data_sent.content: Authorized email and redirect path, consumed magic link or Mparanza session cookie, and ordinary connection metadata

data_sent.when: When requesting taxonomy, evidence packages, or mapping worksets
data_sent.content: Retailer, category, taxonomy version and hash, evidence job and mapping submission identifiers, mapping mode, and explicit correction reason when used

data_sent.when: At the explicit authenticated server-write checkpoint
data_sent.content: Pinned mapping workset identity and hash, idempotency key, complete mapping tasks, Codex decisions, validated mappings, and independent mapping review

data_sent.when: When creating a downstream Brand Fit package
data_sent.content: Actor-owned source evidence-job identifier, brand source retailer, brand name, source Retailer Signals report SHA-256 and verdict, and optional owned or retailer category aliases; the local report file is not sent

data_sent.when: Only when an app_files development run explicitly requests a fresh scrape
data_sent.content: Structured locally captured retailer listing and product-detail records written to the server-backed database; this upload path is not available in the installed Clara plugin

data_returned.when: During Retailer Signals preparation and mapping
data_returned.content: Published category taxonomy, immutable public mapping worksets, task coverage, source identifiers, mapping acceptance receipts, and sanitization/provenance receipts

data_returned.when: After actor-owned evidence jobs are ready
data_returned.content: Checksum-bound structured retail records, cohort comparisons, accepted mapping state, package provenance, and public image URLs; packages contain no server paths or image bytes

data_returned.when: After an actor-owned Brand Fit job is ready
data_returned.content: Checksum-bound current retailer-presence rows, brand-owned catalogue rows, accepted mapping-state snapshot, scope metrics, candidate-product evidence, timestamps, and public image URLs

access.arrangement: The installed bridge uses an authenticated Mparanza session. Artifacts and jobs are actor-owned and another authenticated user receives no information about whether an artifact exists. The plugin receives no database credentials. Development persistence uses the app_files runtime rather than the installed plugin.
controls: The remote bridge accepts only HTTPS mparanza.com or www.mparanza.com outside local tests.
A supplied cookie is attached only to the exact approved origin; a persistent cookie jar must be a private 0600 regular file outside run and repository folders.
Downloads are size-limited, origin-pinned, content-type checked, checksum-verified, and safely extracted.
Mapping writes are transactional, idempotent, and require the complete independently reviewed artifact set.

retention.statement: The bridge prunes evidence jobs after 30 days, mapping worksets after 7 days, and mapping submissions after 180 days, subject to live dependency retention and actor quotas. Those periods do not apply to central structured product records, taxonomy, accepted mappings, retailer-presence data, or owned-catalogue data; their automatic retention schedule is not established by the reviewed source.

security_controls.control: Local Retailer Signals and Brand Fit HTML, report models, semantic reviews, browser QA, and hydrated image bytes are not uploaded by the installed workflows.
Brand Fit sends the source report hash, verdict, and actor-owned evidence-job identifier rather than the report file.
The installed plugin uses the authenticated bridge and never receives direct database credentials.
